target.title: feat(doctor): add Node.js runtime info health contribution
target.body:
## Summary

Add a new Doctor health contribution that displays Node.js runtime
diagnostics during `openclaw doctor`, providing essential context for
troubleshooting and proactive upgrade guidance.

## What it does

**Runtime summary note** (always shown):
Node 24.14.0 · ~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node · via nvm

**Version manager detection** — identifies nvm, fnm, volta, asdf, n,
nodenv, nodebrew, and nvs from the executable path.

**Proactive warnings** (when applicable):
- Node version below minimum (>=22.14.0) — rarely triggers since the
  startup gate catches this first, but guards edge cases
- Node release in maintenance phase — shows months remaining until EOL
- Node release past end-of-life — recommends upgrading to Node 24
- Older-than-recommended LTS — gentle nudge toward Node 24

## Why

Doctor currently has no visibility into the Node.js runtime environment.
When users share Doctor output in issues or with maintainers, the Node
version and installation method are critical diagnostic context that was
previously missing.

This reuses existing infrastructure:
- `runtime-guard.ts` — `detectRuntime()`, `parseSemver()`, `runtimeSatisfies()`
- `runtime-paths.ts` — `isVersionManagedNodePath()`

No new dependencies. No runtime behavior changes outside of Doctor.

## Changes

New files:
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.ts` (~200 lines) — diagnostics
  collection, warning generation, summary formatting, contribution entry
- `src/commands/doctor-node-runtime.test.ts` — 27 unit tests

Modified:
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts` — +1 import, +1 wrapper
  function, +1 contribution registration (after `bundled-plugin-runtime-deps`,
  before `state-integrity`)

## Test plan

- `pnpm check` — 0 errors ✅
- `vitest` — 27/27 tests ✅
- `pnpm build` — clean ✅

Test coverage:
- `detectVersionManagerName`: 11 tests (null, system, nvm, fnm, volta,
  asdf, n, nodenv, nodebrew, nvs, Windows backslash)
- `collectNodeRuntimeDiagnostics`: 3 tests (nvm path, unknown runtime,
  system install)
- `buildNodeRuntimeWarnings`: 8 tests (recommended version, below
  minimum, past EOL, maintenance phase, pre-maintenance nudge, current
  recommended, null major, future major)
- `buildNodeRuntimeSummary`: 5 tests (system install, named manager,
  unknown manager, null version, separator format)

---


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Doctor had no Node.js runtime summary or lifecycle warning. runtime-guard/runtime-paths already expose detection helpers but no Doctor contribution surfaced them. This adds a `doctor:node-runtime` contribution that reports the running Node version, exec path, version-manager, and warns on below-minimum / EOL / maintenance-phase runtimes.

- **Real environment tested:** Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), running Node.js 24.14.0, OpenClaw built from this PR head. Exercised the exported `collectNodeRuntimeDiagnostics()`, `buildNodeRuntimeSummary()`, and `buildNodeRuntimeWarnings()` against this machine's actual runtime plus injected lifecycle scenarios via tsx.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** invoked the patched runtime diagnostic functions via tsx:
```bash
$ node --import tsx run-node-check.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Actual output. (1) is this machine's real runtime; (2)/(3) inject lifecycle scenarios; (2) uses the real current date to compute maintenance window:
```
Real Node.js runtime (this machine):
  summary: Node 24.14.0 · /usr/bin/node · system install
  warnings: (none - supported)

Injected Node 22 LTS (maintenance phase, real current date):
  Node 22 LTS is in maintenance mode (EOL 2027-04-30, ~11 months remaining).
  Consider upgrading to Node 24 for the latest features and longer support.

Injected Node 20.18.0 (below minimum >=22.14.0):
  Node 20.18.0 does not meet the minimum requirement (>=22.14.0).
  Upgrade Node: https://nodejs.org/en/download

Summary variant (nvm-managed path):
  Node 24.14.0 · /home/u/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node · via nvm
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real Node 24.14.0 runtime renders a clean summary with no warning (supported). Injecting Node 22 LTS produces a maintenance-mode warning with a months-remaining estimate computed from the real current date; injecting Node 20 (below the >=22.14.0 minimum) produces a minimum-version warning with an upgrade link. The summary correctly attributes a version-manager (via nvm) when the exec path is under a managed directory.

- **What was not tested:** The full `openclaw doctor` end-to-end TUI invocation was not captured here (it triggers a heavy dist build); the patched diagnostic functions are exercised directly against the real runtime and the same data the Doctor contribution consumes. The recommended-version nudge path was not shown because the only tracked LTS (Node 22) is already in maintenance under the current date, so it takes the maintenance branch.
target.author: alkor2000
